title: Columella wrayi
intro: Columella wrayi, also known as Black Columella or Black Columbella, is a plant species belonging to the family Acanthaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Columella wrayi include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and skin infections.
